blockers.title: node form-data bodies aren't transmitted by the dev FetchHttpClient
blockers.why: Multipart / form-data request bodies arrive empty (dataSize:0) over fetch-http-client.ts — native fetch + a node form-data stream, no Content-Length. Confirmed framework-wide (the human discord_send_message fails identically; the diff vs upstream/main is comment-only). Blocks any atomic that posts a file via form-data.
blockers.fix: Fix the framework HTTP client to transmit node form-data (set Content-Length / use a compatible body), or verify affected atomics in a real flow where a prior step produces the file. discord_send_message already works around it (JSON for text, payload_json for files).

blockers.title: Slack user groups (subteams) — 7 atomics
blockers.why: Slack returns paid_teams_only — user groups are a paid Slack plan feature. The sandbox workspace is free and has zero existing groups, so the dependent atomics have no target.
blockers.fix: Seed a connection to a paid Slack workspace (Pro/Business+), create one test group, then run all 7: create → update / enable / disable / list-members / set-members / find-by-handle.

blockers.title: clickup add_task_to_list
blockers.why: 403 TIML_001 — multi-homing a task into more than one list is a paid ClickUp ClickApp (blocked on free). Confirmed twice; not a code bug.
blockers.fix: Test on a paid workspace with the 'Tasks in Multiple Lists' ClickApp enabled. Endpoint POST /api/v2/list/{list_id}/task/{task_id}.

blockers.title: clickup get_task_time_in_status
blockers.why: 403 TIS_027 — Time in Status is a ClickUp Business+ feature.
blockers.fix: Test on a Business-plan workspace. Endpoint GET /api/v2/task/{id}/time_in_status.

blockers.title: clickup add_guest_to_task / remove_guest_from_task
blockers.why: Guests are an Enterprise ClickUp feature; the sandbox plan has no guest accounts, so there's no real guest_id to add or remove.
blockers.fix: On an Enterprise workspace, invite a guest, capture the guest id, then add → remove against a throwaway task.

blockers.title: resend — entire piece held (34 of 36 atomics)
blockers.why: The seeded Resend connection uses a send-only restricted API key → every read/management atomic returns 401 'restricted to only send emails'. Only send_email + send_batch_emails verified (2/36). Code is sound (Tier-1 green).
blockers.fix: Create a full-access Resend API key, reconnect the connection, then re-run the resend Tier-2 leg (scratchpad/wf-tier2-waveA.js). Audiences/Contacts/Segments/Templates/Broadcasts also need a paid plan.

blockers.title: gmail Tier B — label / trash / modify-labels / batch (11 atomics)
blockers.why: All 11 fail invalid_request — the seeded connection predates the gmail.modify / gmail.labels scopes → re-consent wall. Code is correct + Tier-1 green. Authored on local/ai-actions-dev-page, not in the PR.
blockers.fix: Confirm the scope expansion, reconnect the gmail dev connection (re-consent), then Tier-2 the 11. The settings cluster + People-API contacts need broader / second-API scopes.

blockers.title: google-forms create / edit — 9 atomics
blockers.why: These need the restricted forms.body scope, which Google blocks on the unverified dev/managed OAuth app (hard block, no override). Dropped from the PR; forms ships read-only.
blockers.fix: Gated on Google verifying the forms.body scope for AP's OAuth app; then author / ship the 9 create+edit atomics.

blockers.title: slack get_team_info
blockers.why: team.info needs the team:read OAuth scope, absent from slack auth.ts; the seeded token predates it → missing_scope.
blockers.fix: Add team:read to the bot scope[] in slack/src/lib/auth.ts and reconnect. Belongs in a small dedicated scope-expansion PR.

blockers.title: slack make_file_public / revoke_file_public_url
blockers.why: files.sharedPublicURL / revokePublicURL are user-token-only (bot → not_allowed_token_type); the user token then lacks files:write → missing_scope. Token type already fixed; scope remains.
blockers.fix: Add files:write to the user scope[] in auth.ts and reconnect. Bundle with team:read in one scope-expansion PR. Recover files from commit 0f227ba / ccd7ea3.

blockers.title: discord list_thread_members
blockers.why: GET /channels/{id}/thread-members returns 403 Missing Access without the privileged GUILD_MEMBERS gateway intent — permission/intent gate, not a code defect.
blockers.fix: Enable the GUILD_MEMBERS privileged intent on the bot, then test on a thread the bot has joined.

blockers.title: clickup set_custom_field_value / remove_custom_field_value
blockers.why: No custom field exists in the sandbox, and ClickUp has no public API to create one (UI-only) → there's no field_id to target.
blockers.fix: In the ClickUp UI add a custom field, run get_accessible_custom_fields for its id, then set → remove on a throwaway task. Endpoints POST/DELETE /api/v2/task/{id}/field/{field_id}.

blockers.title: clickup create_list_from_template
blockers.why: No list template exists in the workspace (templates are created in the UI).
blockers.fix: Save any list as a template in the ClickUp UI, then test with that template_id against a folder/space; delete the created list after.

blockers.title: slack upload_file
blockers.why: Its file input is a Property.File; the Tier-2 test-step harness can't synthesize / inject a file object. It's a twin of the production-shipped uploadFile action.
blockers.fix: Verify inside a real flow where a prior step produces the file, or via the UI flow builder with a manual upload.

blockers.title: slack unfurl_url
blockers.why: chat.unfurl requires a pending link_shared event (the app must own the link's domain); it can't be synthesized by a test-step → cannot_unfurl_url.
blockers.fix: Register the app for a domain, post a link that triggers link_shared, then call with that event's channel + ts. Niche — consider leaving deferred.

blockers.title: trello card attachments — add / get / delete
blockers.why: add_card_attachment is file-only (Property.File, required) → needs multipart, which the dev httpClient can't transmit and the harness can't inject (same form-data limit). The byte-identical human action fails the same. get/delete are code-correct but have no attachment id to chain.
blockers.fix: Verify in a real flow where a prior step produces the file, or on an env whose httpClient sends node form-data. For get/delete, attach a link via the Trello UI/API first to obtain an attachment_id.

blockers.title: apify delete_dataset
blockers.why: Destructive; not run live to avoid deleting real data. Code path verified Tier-1.
blockers.fix: Test on a throwaway dataset (create via a hello-world run → delete).

blockers.title: firecrawl get_extract_results
blockers.why: No way to obtain a standalone extract job id (extract_data polls inline + strips the id; no list-extracts endpoint), and Firecrawl reports /v2/extract/{id} as deprecated. The endpoint path is correct.
blockers.fix: Call with a real extract id captured out-of-band, or migrate to the /v2/scrape json-format replacement on next re-author.

blockers.title: slack unarchive_channel
blockers.why: Was not_in_channel on the bot token (Slack only lets a member/admin user token unarchive).
blockers.fix: Swapped getBotToken → requireUserToken (same pattern as delete-message / search-messages). Verified live: create → archive → unarchive → ok:true. Now shipping (slack 43 → 44).

blockers.title: discord send_message — form-data transport
blockers.why: The twin (and the human action) always sent form-data multipart even for text → broken transport (empty body).
blockers.fix: Rewrote the twin to send a JSON body for text and payload_json multipart only when files are attached. Text path live-verified 6/6.

blockers.title: Brevo (sendinblue) — entire piece held (20 atomics)
blockers.why: The seeded Brevo connection's API key returns 401 'Key not found' (revoked / invalid / expired) on every call → 0/20 verifiable. Auth wiring is correct (api-key header transmitted, confirmed in the request body that reached Brevo) and the code is Tier-1 green; the actions surface a clean 'Brevo authentication failed' message. Same class as the resend key blocker.
blockers.fix: Create / re-seed a valid Brevo project API key, reconnect the connection, then re-run the sendinblue Tier-2 leg (scratchpad/wf-tier2-w3.js). 6 of the 20 (SMS + CRM-companies) also need those Brevo add-ons enabled.

blockers.title: stripe disputes & payouts — 4 atomics
blockers.why: get_payout / get_dispute / update_dispute have no live resource on a fresh test account (no payouts, no chargebacks — neither can be created on demand); close_dispute is irreversible (concedes + forfeits funds) so it was never live-fired. All returned clean API round-trips / correct 404s — code is sound.
blockers.fix: On an account with a real payout cycle + a test dispute, run get_payout / get_dispute / update_dispute; live-fire close_dispute only against a throwaway disputed charge you are willing to concede.

blockers.title: airtable create_base_ai
blockers.why: Airtable exposes no list-workspaces API, so there is no obtainable workspace_id to create a base under. The schema.bases:write scope IS present (the sibling schema-write atomics passed live).
blockers.fix: Capture a workspace_id from the Airtable UI URL (wsp...), then run create_base_ai with it. Endpoint POST /v0/meta/bases.

blockers.title: github request_pull_request_reviewers / create_organization_repository
blockers.why: request_reviewers → 422 'cannot request review on your own PR' (no second collaborator on the account); create_organization_repository → 403 (the OAuth account has no org with write access). Both mapped the vendor error cleanly — not code defects.
blockers.fix: Add a second collaborator to the test repo to request a review; create the org repo against an org where the connected account has write access.

blockers.title: cody upload_file_to_kb
blockers.why: Composite (mint signed URL → raw S3 PUT → register) needing a Property.File input; the Tier-2 test-step harness can't synthesize / inject a file object. Tier-1 green. Same class as slack upload_file / trello attachments.
blockers.fix: Verify inside a real flow where a prior step produces the file, or via the UI flow builder with a manual upload.

blockers.title: pubrio delete_monitor
blockers.why: Both the pre-existing endpoint (POST /monitors/delete) and pubrio's own documented replacement (DELETE /monitors/{id}) 404 in production — a vendor-side docs-vs-prod drift, not a code defect. Kept audience:'human'; agents can already deactivate a monitor via update_monitor_ai.
blockers.fix: Retest once pubrio fixes either delete route; there is no code-side workaround.

blockers.title: quizell get_customer_by_id / create_customer_lead / update_customer_record
blockers.why: create/update need a quiz_id with no in-piece resolver (no list-quizzes-by-customer style endpoint), and the sandbox test account has no existing customer to fetch by id — none are verifiable as authored. Demoted to audience:'human' (2026-06-30 Greptile pass).
blockers.fix: Capture a real quiz_id from the Quizell UI/API and a seeded customer record, then Tier-2 all 3 before considering an 'ai' twin.

blockers.title: MCP redesign spike
blockers.why: The MCP server exposes ~40 generic ap_* tools (the action is a parameter to ap_run_action), not one tool per action — nothing reads audience/aiMetadata and there's no per-action slot. The premise behind Phase-1 tasks #4–#6 (collapse 446 custom_api_call tools / per-action tools/list) doesn't hold.
blockers.fix: Run the spike: decide how audience/aiMetadata surface through the discovery tools (ap_research_pieces, ap_get_piece_props) + ap_run_action. Blocks tasks #4–#6 (#3 aiReady is independent).

blockers.title: Engine-required action filter
blockers.why: 127 engine-required actions (97 AI-relevant across ~58 pieces) are advertised to the agent surface even though they hard-fail at runtime.
blockers.fix: Pull a minimal read-only filter (or a static deny-set seeded from engine_required_actions.csv) into the Phase-1 MCP work.

blockers.title: Output-contract feature — owner / ETA
blockers.why: Phase-3/4 + the MCP outputSchema slot depend on a separate output-contract feature with no PR# / owner / ETA. outputSchema was correctly removed from aiMetadata to avoid two sources of truth.
blockers.fix: Get a PR# / owner / ETA for the output-contract feature before Phase 3 depends on it, or restore a minimal interim outputSchema.

blockers.title: Path D drop rationale (owed)
blockers.why: Pure Path A was adopted, but the written rationale for dropping the prior A+D+B recommendation is still owed; CLAUDE.md conflates Path C and Path D.
blockers.fix: Add an explicit written decision record (accept the measured efficiency loss for maintenance / IP / single-source reasons); fix the CLAUDE.md C-vs-D line.

blockers.title: Deferred-dependency table in PROGRESS
blockers.why: The deferral chain (output-shape, engineCoupling, error-envelope, Phase 4) is load-bearing and external, with no consolidated table.
blockers.fix: Add a dependency table to PROGRESS with PR# / owner / ETA; gate the consuming phases.
